summary: Good audience recording of a fiery June‑29 1980 show with a scorching Scarlet > Fire and tight Stranger, though the tape lacks high‑end sparkle.
review: The Mason audience tape captures an electric 6/29/80 performance – Jerry in hyperdrive on Scarlet > Fire and the band delivering a tribal‑beat‑driven Stranger that fans still cite. The recording isn’t perfect; it’s missing some high‑frequency detail, has a little hiss and a brief cut, but listeners consider it one of the better versions available.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the show (4‑5 ★) and highlight standout songs, while noting recording shortcomings; the weighted average yields a solid but not outstanding rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Missing high‑end detail, some hiss, occasional cut but overall clear enough; considered the best available Mason tap of this show.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as "full on" and second set ignited a fired‑up crowd; the energy builds to a climactic Scarlet > Fire that blew the roof off.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Hyperdrive guitar work on Scarlet > Fire, tight tribal beat on Stranger.
band_member_comments.Bob: Airborne vocals and rhythm during the show.
